author: opus-orchestrator <t@t.com>
body:
## Findings

User chose option B: leave the migration script gitignored.

## Recommendation

Close as resolved-by-design. The spec was correct — one-shot
operational scripts don't deserve in-tree maintenance burden.
The `refs/jjf/*` commit log captures the planner mutations
(64→0 epic:* labels, 9→0 bare epic, 20 parent-child edges,
16 self-attribution strips); that IS the audit trail. The
script remains recoverable from local backup at
`experiments/drop-epic-labels/run.sh` if anyone needs to
replay the pattern.

## Confidence

High. User decision, recorded.

## Open follow-ups

None. If a similar migration is needed in another jjforge-
hosted repo, the self-attribution skip-branch insight is the
load-bearing novelty; re-derive from this commit history or
ping the original session.
